title: use golangci-lint
body:
Other projects (e.g., CRI-O and Buildah) either use or pass golangci-lint runs already and it has shown to improve the quality and maintainability of the code quite a bit (did not quantify).

Before a migration would be possible, the code has to be cleaned up. Below you can find a run on current master:
```
contrib/perftest/main.go:183:26: CreateConfigToOCISpec not declared by package createconfig (typecheck)
		runtimeSpec, err := cc.CreateConfigToOCISpec(config)
		                       ^
contrib/perftest/main.go:72:39: can only use ... with matching parameter (typecheck)
	client, err := libpod.NewRuntime(opts...)
	                                     ^
contrib/perftest/main.go:120:41: cannot convert nil (untyped nil value) to namespaces.UsernsMode (typecheck)
	idmappings, err := util.ParseIDMapping(nil, nil, "", "")
	                                       ^
dependencies/dependencies.go:4:2: import "github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/onsi/ginkgo/ginkgo" is a program, not an importable package (typecheck)
	_ "github.com/onsi/ginkgo/ginkgo"
	^
dependencies/dependencies.go:5:2: import "github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/varlink/go/cmd/varlink-go-interface-generator" is a program, not an importable package (typecheck)
	_ "github.com/varlink/go/cmd/varlink-go-interface-generator" // Note: this file is used to trick `go mod` into vendoring the command below.
	^
```

comments.body:
We rolled this out once, but had to immediately revert because it completely hosed our CI.

I'm fine with giving it another go, but this time we need to make sure the testing images (in particular ci/prow/lint) are ready.
